Example Page

2021 Cabernet Sauvignon Beauregard Ranch

Beauregard Vineyards was founded in 1945 by Amos and Cecilia Beauregard. Five generations later, the vines now span 60 acres in Bonny Doon California, where coastal wind, sandstone, and Zayante sand give Cabernet a more tensile, mountain-grown profile.

Scores & Accolades

96 Points - Vinous

92 Points - Wine Enthusiast

Estate release | Pricing on request

Current Release

Dark-fruited Cabernet shaped by sandstone, Zayante sand, and the cooling pull of the Pacific.

Estate Release

2021 Cabernet Sauvignon Beauregard Ranch

Pricing on request | 750 ml | limited mountain allocation

Wine Specs

Estate detail with mountain precision

Beauregard Ranch Vineyard

Acres: 10

Vine age: 2005

Elevation: 1,740 to 1,802 feet

Site

Soil: Zayante Sand

Bedrock: Sand stone

Distance to Pacific Ocean: 4.56 miles

Ingredients

Estate grown Cabernet Sauvignon grapes

Indigenous yeast and naturally occurring malolactic bacteria

Minimal sulphur dioxide, aged in French oak barrels

Winemaker notes

Mountain structure, framed like a magazine story

The Beauregard Ranch sits at an elevation where the vines are forced to struggle through ancient marine soils, cooled by Pacific air just miles away. The 2021 vintage delivered a Cabernet with a clear structural point of view: firm tannins, bright acidity, and a more tensile expression than the usual broad, plush valley style.

  • In the Glass: Dark, impenetrable garnet.
  • The Nose: Crushed graphite, damp redwood needles, wild mountain mint, cassis, and dark cherry.
  • The Palate: Lean and muscular, with blackcurrant, dark plum, cedar box, tobacco leaf, espresso, fine-grained tannins, and an electric spine of acidity.

DTC Focus

From score sheet to cellar intent.

For the drinker who wants mountain Cabernet with precision rather than bulk, the route is clear: read the site, trust the critics, and move straight into an allocation request without leaving the estate story behind.

Grown, Produced & Bottled by Beauregard Vineyards, Santa Cruz, CA

Built to balance estate heritage, terroir detail, and a direct allocation path without losing the quiet restraint of a Santa Cruz Mountains release page.
